top_signals.title: Dangote Breaks Ground on 700,000-Bpd Second Crude Processing Unit
top_signals.summary: Africa's biggest refinery is getting bigger. Nigeria's Dangote refinery has started work on a second crude processing unit that will add another 700,000 barrels per day (bpd) of capacity, putting the complex on track to rival the world's largest refining site and giving billionaire owner Aliko Dangote a much larger role in global fuel markets. According to Dangote Petroleum Refinery CEO David Bird, construction is already underway at the Lekki site outside Lagos. The new refinery is expected to be online by the end of 2028 and would lift total…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Palcos del Mundial en México desatan un mercado informal y millonario
top_signals.summary: Mientras millones de aficionados buscan entradas para el Mundial, en Ciudad de México se mueve un negocio reservado para unos pocos. Palcos con vista privilegiada se ofrecen por hasta 27 millones de pesos en un mercado paralelo que opera por WhatsApp y que enfrenta a propietarios, intermediarios, la FIFA y los administradores del Estadio Banorte. (Source: Bloomberg)

top_signals.title: Inside the $850 million Obama Presidential Center ahead of its Juneteenth opening
top_signals.summary: President Obama's library cost $30 to visit, making it the most expensive presidential museum or library in modern history.

top_signals.title: Fuji Media’s real estate unit attracts ¥1 trillion bids
top_signals.summary: More than 15 firms submitted bids for Sankei Building last month, with several offers exceeding ¥1 trillion ($6.3 billion).

top_signals.title: UN food agency discloses breach affecting 600,000 Gaza households
top_signals.summary: The United Nations' World Food Programme (WFP), the world's largest humanitarian organization, revealed over the weekend that its self-registration application (SRA) for Palestine was breached. [...]
